connectorUiConfig.title: AWS EKS Data Connector (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The AWS EKS data connector provides the capability to ingest logs from [Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service](https://aws.amazon.com/eks/) into Microsoft Sentinel. This connector allows you to receive and process messages from your SQS queues, making them available for security monitoring and analysis. The connector supports both standard and FIFO queues and provides comprehensive message metadata including attributes, timestamps, and sender information.

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of AWS EKS messages

dataTypes.name: AWSEKSLogs_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
AWSEKSLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Configuration
instructionSteps.description: Configure the AWS EKS connector with your queue details and credentials.

parameters.content:
> [!NOTE]
> This data connector requires AWS credentials with appropriate SQS permissions to receive messages from your queues.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Ensure your AWS credentials have the following SQS permissions:
> - `sqs:ReceiveMessage`
> - `sqs:DeleteMessage` (if deleteMessageAfterReceive is enabled)
> - `sqs:GetQueueAttributes`

parameters.label: Queue URL
parameters.placeholder: The full AWS EKS queue URL (e.g., https://sqs.region.amazonaws.com/account-id/queue-name)
parameters.name: queueUrl

parameters.label: Role ARN
parameters.placeholder: The AWS IAM Role ARN for cross-account access (e.g., arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/SentinelRole)
parameters.name: roleArn

parameters.content: - **Max Messages**: Number of messages to retrieve per polling operation (1-10, default: 10)
- **Visibility Timeout**: Duration (in seconds) that messages are hidden from other consumers (0-43200, default: 30)
- **Wait Time**: Long polling wait time in seconds (0-20, default: 20)
- **Delete After Receive**: Whether to automatically delete messages after processing (default: true)
